authoritative_sources.title: Regulation 22: Race Books and Sports Pools
authoritative_sources.publisher: Nevada Gaming Commission / Nevada Gaming Control Board
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Nevada's sportsbook regulation is a primary public operating rule for sportsbook terminology, wager acceptance, wagering accounts, suspicious transaction reporting, and house-rule boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Lane-wide sportsbook control framing and wager-account boundary review

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Internal Control Standards: Race and Sports
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Nevada MICS are authoritative control standards for sportsbook ticketing, payout and redemption, wagering multiple transaction logs, liability records, and reconciliation expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Ticket issuance, payout evidence, and reconciliation handling

authoritative_sources.title: Chapter 69N: Sports Wagering
authoritative_sources.publisher: New Jersey Division of Gaming Enforcement
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: New Jersey DGE's sports wagering regulation is a primary public rule source for sportsbook tickets, vouchers, kiosks, house rules, rescission controls, and recordkeeping in a major regulated market.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Retail and mobile sportsbook execution plus rescission and redemption handling

authoritative_sources.title: Frequently Asked Questions Casino Recordkeeping, Reporting, and Compliance Program Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FinCEN is the primary U.S. federal AML authority for casinos and this official FAQ is authoritative for CTR, SAR, kiosk, wagering-account, and compliance-program boundaries relevant to sportsbook operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cash, account, and AML prerequisite review

authoritative_sources.title: Guidance on Preparing a Complete and Sufficient Suspicious Activity Report Narrative
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FinCEN's SAR narrative guidance is the authoritative public workflow reference for suspicious-activity evidence preservation and for distinguishing sportsbook operations packaging from formal SAR ownership.

authoritative_sources.title: GLI-33 Event Wagering Systems
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GLI-33 is the recognized vendor-neutral event-wagering standard used across gaming jurisdictions for wagering-account transactions, audit logging, settlement, voids, and operational controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Sportsbook system workflow, account transactions, and audit traceability

authoritative_sources.title: Internet Responsible Gambling Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NCPG's internet responsible gambling standards are a recognized public control reference for self-exclusion, player limit tools, messaging, and escalation expectations in online wagering environments.

workflow_stages.name: Sportsbook intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the ticket or wagering account, jurisdiction, channel, player eligibility, event eligibility, and governing house-rule context before sportsbook work progresses.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Wager review, grading, and payout-readiness handling
workflow_stages.description: Use ticket, line-history, event-result, account, and payout records to decide whether the sportsbook item can settle, hold, rework, or route elsewhere.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue and reconciliation handling
workflow_stages.description: Use sportsbook liabilities, payout records, voucher activity, and ledger outputs to track unresolved variances, reversals, redemptions, and rework states.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion and fulfillment evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Require sportsbook-specific proof of settlement, payout, redemption, or account disposition before the item can be completed.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: AML, responsible-gaming, and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when suspicious activity, cash-reporting triggers, exclusion controls, or regulator-sensitive conflicts materially change whether sportsbook work can continue.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: Sportsbook tickets, bet slips, vouchers, and redemption records
Wagering-account ledgers, deposits, withdrawals, and hold or adjustment records
House rules, approved event lists, approved wager types, and line-history logs
Event result feeds, grading records, settlement outcomes, and void or rescission approvals
Cage, cashiering, kiosk, and liability reconciliation records
KYC, age, geolocation, excluded-person, self-exclusion, limit-setting, and responsible-gaming records
AML alerts, surveillance notes, suspicious-activity documentation, and escalation packets
systems_of_record: sportsbook platform or event wagering system
player account management and KYC platform
cage, cashiering, kiosk, and voucher systems
surveillance, AML, and compliance case systems

allowed: Assess whether documented sportsbook records support advancing, holding, reworking, or completing a sportsbook queue item.
Prepare evidence-backed execution updates, completion records, exception notes, and escalation packages tied to tickets, wagering accounts, house rules, and reconciliation evidence.
Request missing prerequisites and record-of-truth evidence before changing lifecycle state.
escalate: Suspicious cash, account, or wagering patterns are present.
Self-exclusion, underage, jurisdiction, geolocation, or responsible-gaming signals change the answer.
House rules, public regulation, tenant SOPs, or systems of record conflict materially.
Trading, risk, cage approval, compliance, legal, or regulator-facing ownership is needed to resolve the item.
refuse: Requests to set lines, approve new markets, or change event outcomes
Requests to void, settle, or pay a wager without authoritative source evidence
Requests for legal interpretation, SAR filing, AML disposition, or responsible-gaming adjudication
Requests that treat sportsbook work as generic casino operations without sportsbook-specific records

domain_failure_modes: Settling, voiding, or paying a wager without authoritative ticket, line-history, event, account, or approval evidence
Treating a suspicious cash or wagering pattern as ordinary customer service work in order to close a queue item quickly
Closing a payout, redemption, or liability break without sportsbook-specific reconciliation and source-of-truth validation
Ignoring player eligibility, self-exclusion, age, or geolocation controls because the request sits in a sportsbook operations queue
Flattening sportsbook work into generic casino operations language that omits tickets, wagering accounts, house rules, event grading, and liability records

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific cancellation, rescission, and redemption differences
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Sportsbook cancellation windows, redemption periods, voucher handling, and account funding rules vary materially by jurisdiction and operator approval model.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require runtime retrieval of governing jurisdiction, channel, and tenant house rules before final rescission, cancellation, payout, or completion decisions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific delegation for cage, compliance, and sportsbook management approvals
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Public baselines define control boundaries, but tenant governance decides which payout, adjustment, or exception actions sportsbook operations may execute directly.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Retrieve the tenant authority matrix and keep outputs recommendation-only whenever approval ownership is unresolved.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because the remaining ambiguity is explicitly constrained to jurisdiction and tenant retrieval at runtime.
The research output constrains the lane to sportsbook execution-state management with explicit cash, account, AML, and responsible-gaming escalation discipline.
